title: Avoid weak SSL/TLS protocols
description: Using weak SSL/TLS protocols like SSLv2, SSLv3, or early TLS versions exposes applications to known cryptographic vulnerabilities. These protocols have security flaws that can be exploited by attackers to intercept or manipulate communications.
recommendation: Use only strong, modern TLS versions (TLS 1.2 or higher) and disable support for weak protocols.

noncompliant.description: Using weak SSL/TLS protocols and cipher suites exposes applications to known cryptographic vulnerabilities and attacks.

compliant.description: Using TLS 1.2 or higher with strong cipher suites ensures secure communication and protects against known cryptographic attacks.

checks.message: Weak SSL/TLS protocol or cipher suite detected. Use TLS 1.2+ with strong AEAD ciphers.
